151.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'When two Muslims fight each other, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire.'
151.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling bertarung, maka si pembunuh dan yang terbunuh sama-sama di neraka.'

152.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Men from those who accompanied me and saw me will certainly come to me at the Hawd. But when they are presented before me and I see them, they will be seized away from me. I will surely say: My Lord, my companions! And it will be said: You do not know what they introduced after you.'
152.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sungguh orang-orang dari mereka yang menemaniku dan melihatku pasti akan datang kepadaku di telaga. Tetapi ketika mereka dihadapkan kepadaku dan aku melihat mereka, mereka akan direnggut dariku. Aku pasti akan berkata: Ya Tuhanku, sahabat-sahabatku! Dan akan dikatakan: Engkau tidak tahu apa yang mereka adakan setelahmu.'

153.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever honors the authority of Allah in this world, Allah will honor him on the Day of Resurrection. And whoever dishonors the authority of Allah in this world, Allah will dishonor him on the Day of Resurrection."'
153.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa memuliakan kekuasaan Allah di dunia, Allah akan memuliakannya pada Hari Kiamat. Dan barangsiapa merendahkan kekuasaan Allah di dunia, Allah akan merendahkannya pada Hari Kiamat."'

154.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from exchanging silver for silver and gold for gold except equal for equal, and he commanded us to exchange silver for gold and gold for silver however we wished. Thabit ibn 'Abdillah said: 'Hand to hand?' He said: 'That is how I heard it.'
154.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami menukar perak dengan perak dan emas dengan emas kecuali sama rata, dan beliau memerintahkan kami menukar perak dengan emas dan emas dengan perak sesuka kami. Tsabit ibn 'Abdillah berkata: 'Langsung dari tangan ke tangan?' Ia berkata: 'Begitulah yang aku dengar.'

155.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ led us in the prayer of fear. He prayed two rak'ahs with some of his companions, then gave the salutation. They moved back and another group came and took their place. He prayed two rak'ahs with them, then gave the salutation. So the Prophet ﷺ prayed four rak'ahs in total and each group prayed two rak'ahs.'
155.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ mengimami kami shalat khauf. Beliau shalat dua rakaat bersama sebagian sahabatnya, kemudian salam. Mereka mundur dan kelompok lain datang menggantikan posisi mereka. Beliau shalat dua rakaat bersama mereka, kemudian salam. Sehingga Nabi ﷺ shalat empat rakaat total dan masing-masing kelompok shalat dua rakaat.'

156.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon on the Day of Sacrifice and said: 'What day is this?' — or said: 'Do you know what day this is?' We said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'Is this not the day of greater Hajj?' and he spoke about the sanctity of this day, month, and city.
156.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah pada Hari Nahr dan bersabda: 'Hari apa ini?' — atau bersabda: 'Apakah kalian tahu hari apa ini?' Kami berkata: 'Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini hari Haji Akbar?' dan beliau berbicara tentang kesucian hari ini, bulan ini, dan kota ini.'

157.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that one day while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was delivering a sermon, al-Hasan ibn 'Ali came and climbed up the pulpit to him. The Prophet ﷺ embraced him and wiped over his head and said: 'This son of mine is a master, and perhaps Allah will reconcile through his hands two factions of Muslims.'
157.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa suatu hari ketika Rasulullah ﷺ sedang berkhutbah, al-Hasan ibn 'Ali datang dan naik ke mimbar menghampirinya. Nabi ﷺ merangkulnya dan mengusap kepalanya dan bersabda: 'Putraku ini adalah seorang pemimpin, dan mungkin Allah akan mendamaikan melalui tangannya dua faksi dari kaum Muslim.'

158.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, which of people is the best?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' He said: 'And which of people is the worst?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
158.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang terbaik?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ia berkata: 'Dan siapakah manusia yang terburuk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

159.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The two months of Eid do not diminish — Ramadan and Dhu al-Hijja.'
159.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dua bulan Eid tidak berkurang — Ramadan dan Dzulhijjah.'

160.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever honors the authority of Allah in this world, Allah will honor him on the Day of Resurrection. And whoever dishonors the authority of Allah in this world, Allah will dishonor him on the Day of Resurrection.'
160.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memuliakan kekuasaan Allah di dunia, Allah akan memuliakannya pada Hari Kiamat. Dan barangsiapa merendahkan kekuasaan Allah di dunia, Allah akan merendahkannya pada Hari Kiamat.'

161.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not turn back after me into disbelievers, striking one another's necks.'
161.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian kembali setelahku menjadi orang-orang kafir yang saling memukul leher satu sama lain.'

162.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A people who are ruled by a woman will never succeed.'
162.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum yang diperintah oleh seorang wanita tidak akan pernah berhasil.'

163.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from exchanging silver for silver and gold for gold except equal for equal, but commanded us to exchange silver for gold and gold for silver however we wished.
163.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami menukar perak dengan perak dan emas dengan emas kecuali sama rata, tetapi memerintahkan kami menukar perak dengan emas dan emas dengan perak sesuka kami.'

164.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Seek Laylat al-Qadr in the last ten nights of Ramadan on the odd-numbered nights.'
164.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Carilah Lailatul Qadar pada sepuluh malam terakhir Ramadan pada malam-malam ganjil.'

165.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no sin more deserving of having its punishment hastened for its perpetrator in this world, along with what is stored for him in the Hereafter, than transgression and severing ties of kinship.'
165.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada dosa yang lebih layak untuk dipercepat hukumannya bagi pelakunya di dunia, beserta apa yang disimpan untuknya di akhirat, selain kezaliman dan memutus silaturahmi.'

166.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Aslam, Ghifar, Muzayna, and Juhayna are better than Banu Tamim, Banu 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'a, and Banu 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan by the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
166.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aslam, Ghifar, Muzaynah, dan Juhaynah lebih baik dari Bani Tamim, Bani 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'ah, dan Bani 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan menurut Rasulullah ﷺ.'

167.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ led them in the prayer of fear: two rak'ahs with one group and two rak'ahs with another group, so the Prophet ﷺ prayed four rak'ahs and they each prayed two rak'ahs.
167.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengimami mereka shalat khauf: dua rakaat bersama satu kelompok dan dua rakaat bersama kelompok lain, sehingga Nabi ﷺ shalat empat rakaat sedangkan mereka masing-masing dua rakaat.'

168.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims face each other with their swords and one kills the other, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is the killer — but what about the killed?' He said: 'Because he intended to kill his companion.'
168.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling berhadapan dengan pedang mereka dan salah satunya membunuh yang lain, maka si pembunuh dan yang terbunuh sama-sama di neraka.' Mereka berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, ini si pembunuh — tapi mengapa yang terbunuh?' Beliau bersabda: 'Karena ia berniat membunuh temannya.'

169.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout all of Ramadan.'
169.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan semuanya.'

170.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A judge must not judge between two people while he is angry.'
170.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang hakim tidak boleh memutuskan perkara antara dua orang ketika ia sedang marah.'

171.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that Jibreel (peace be upon him) said: 'O Muhammad, recite the Quran in one dialect.' Mika'il (peace be upon him) said: 'Ask for more.' He asked for more. He said: 'Recite it in two dialects.' Mika'il said: 'Ask for more.' He kept asking until they reached seven dialects. He said: 'Each one is sufficient and complete, as long as an ayah of mercy is not ended with an ayah of punishment.'
171.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Jibril (alaihissalam) berkata: 'Wahai Muhammad, bacalah Al-Qur'an dalam satu dialek.' Mikail (alaihissalam) berkata: 'Mintalah tambahan.' Ia meminta tambahan. Beliau bersabda: 'Bacalah dalam dua dialek.' Mikail berkata: 'Mintalah tambahan.' Ia terus meminta hingga mencapai tujuh dialek. Beliau bersabda: 'Masing-masing cukup dan sempurna, selama ayat rahmat tidak diakhiri dengan ayat azab.'

172.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty without just cause will not smell the fragrance of Paradise, even though its fragrance can be detected from a distance of a hundred years' journey.'
172.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seorang yang berada dalam perjanjian tanpa alasan yang benar, ia tidak akan mencium wangi surga, padahal wanginya dapat tercium dari jarak perjalanan seratus tahun.'

173.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was praying, and when he prostrated, al-Hasan would jump onto his back and neck. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ would rise gently so as not to cause him to fall. He did this more than once. When he finished his prayer, they said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we saw you do something with al-Hasan.' He said: 'This son of mine is a master.'
173.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ sedang shalat, dan ketika sujud, al-Hasan melompat ke punggung dan lehernya. Rasulullah ﷺ bangkit dengan lembut agar al-Hasan tidak jatuh. Ia melakukan ini lebih dari sekali. Ketika beliau selesai shalat, mereka berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, kami melihat engkau melakukan sesuatu dengan al-Hasan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Putraku ini adalah seorang pemimpin.'

174.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A people who are ruled by a woman will never succeed.'
174.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum yang diperintah oleh seorang wanita tidak akan pernah berhasil.'

175.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims face each other with their swords, both of them intending to kill the other, and one kills the other, both are in the Fire.' It was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is the killer — but what about the killed?' He said: 'Because he intended to kill his companion.'
175.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling berhadapan dengan pedang mereka, keduanya berniat membunuh yang lain, dan salah satunya membunuh yang lain, keduanya di neraka.' Ditanyakan: 'Ya Rasulullah, ini si pembunuh — tapi mengapa yang terbunuh?' Beliau bersabda: 'Karena ia berniat membunuh temannya.'

176.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims face each other with their swords and one kills the other, both the killer and the killed are in the Fire together.'
176.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling berhadapan dengan pedang mereka dan salah satunya membunuh yang lain, maka si pembunuh dan yang terbunuh sama-sama di neraka bersama.'

177.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that one day the Messenger of Allah ﷺ described the Dajjal and the description of his parents. He said: 'The parents of the Dajjal will remain childless for thirty years, then a son will be born to them — circumcised, the least beneficial of things and the most harmful. His eyes sleep but his heart does not sleep.' Then he described them further.
177.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa suatu hari Rasulullah ﷺ menggambarkan Dajjal dan ciri-ciri orang tuanya. Beliau bersabda: 'Orang tua Dajjal akan tetap tanpa anak selama tiga puluh tahun, kemudian lahirlah bagi mereka seorang putra — yang disunat, yang paling sedikit manfaatnya dan paling berbahaya. Matanya tidur namun hatinya tidak tidur.' Kemudian beliau menggambarkan mereka lebih lanjut.'

178.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout all of Ramadan.' Qatada said: Allah knows best — whether he feared self-praise for his nation, or he said: 'There must inevitably be one who sleeps or is heedless.'
178.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan semuanya.' Qatadah berkata: Allah lebih mengetahui — apakah beliau khawatir akan pujian diri bagi umatnya, atau beliau berkata: 'Pasti ada yang tidur atau lalai.'

179.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he wrote to his son who was a governor in Sijistan: 'Do not judge between two men while you are angry, for I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "No judge should judge between two people or two disputants while he is angry."'
179.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menulis kepada putranya yang menjadi gubernur di Sijistan: 'Jangan memutuskan perkara antara dua orang ketika engkau sedang marah, karena aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Tidak boleh seorang hakim memutuskan perkara antara dua orang atau dua pihak yang berselisih ketika ia sedang marah."'

180.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty without just cause, Allah, Blessed and Exalted, has indeed forbidden him Paradise — he will not smell its fragrance.'
180.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seorang yang berada dalam perjanjian tanpa alasan yang benar, Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi sungguh telah mengharamkan surga baginya — ia tidak akan mencium wanginya.'

181.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that there was a story in which 'Abdullah was given a choice between thirty thousand and some silver vessels. He chose the vessels. Then merchants came from Darin and he sold the vessels to them, ten for thirteen. He then met Abu Bakra and said: 'Did you not see how I deceived them?' He said: 'How?' He mentioned it to him, and Abu Bakra said: 'I swear by Allah, I will not speak to you.'
181.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ada sebuah kisah di mana 'Abdullah diberi pilihan antara tiga puluh ribu dan beberapa bejana perak. Ia memilih bejana. Kemudian para pedagang datang dari Darin dan ia menjual bejana kepada mereka, sepuluh dengan harga tiga belas. Ia kemudian bertemu Abu Bakra dan berkata: 'Tidakkah kamu lihat bagaimana aku menipu mereka?' Ia berkata: 'Bagaimana?' Ia menceritakannya, dan Abu Bakra berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak akan berbicara denganmu.'

182.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A Muhajir may stay in Makkah after completing his Hajj for three days.'
182.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang Muhajir boleh tinggal di Makkah setelah menyelesaikan hajinya selama tiga hari.'

183.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said to the Muhajir: 'Three days after the departure' — meaning three days stay after completing the Hajj rituals.
183.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ bersabda kepada para Muhajir: 'Tiga hari setelah keberangkatan' — yakni tiga hari tinggal setelah menyelesaikan ritual haji.'

184.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent me to Bahrain — or to the people of Hajar — Abu Hamza doubted. He said: I used to come to a walled garden belonging to brothers who disputed about it.'
184.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-'Ala' ibn al-Hadrami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengutusku ke Bahrain — atau kepada penduduk Hajar — Abu Hamzah ragu. Ia berkata: Aku biasa mendatangi sebuah kebun milik saudara-saudara yang berselisih tentangnya.'

185.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man who said: 'I was in a gathering in which 'Umar ibn al-Khattab was present in Madinah. 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said to a man among his companions: "How did you hear the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say it?" He said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: Islam began young, then as a two-year-old, then as a four-year-old."'
185.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang lelaki yang berkata: 'Aku berada dalam sebuah majelis yang di dalamnya ada 'Umar ibn al-Khatthab di Madinah. 'Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata kepada seorang lelaki di antara sahabat duduknya: "Bagaimana kamu mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ mengatakannya?" Ia berkata: "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Islam dimulai seperti anak domba muda, kemudian seperti yang berumur dua tahun, kemudian seperti yang berumur empat tahun."'

186.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We came to the Prophet ﷺ as young men and stayed with him for about twenty nights. He said to us: "If you return to your lands" — and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was compassionate — "teach them." He said: He commanded us to pray, and he described how to pray.',
186.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith al-Laythi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami datang kepada Nabi ﷺ sebagai pemuda dan tinggal bersamanya sekitar dua puluh malam. Beliau berkata kepada kami: "Jika kalian kembali ke tanah kalian" — dan Rasulullah ﷺ adalah orang yang penyayang — "ajarkanlah mereka." Ia berkata: Beliau memerintahkan kami untuk shalat, dan beliau menggambarkan cara shalat.'

187.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith, who is Abu Sulayman, that he and a companion — or two companions — came to the Prophet ﷺ. He said to them: 'When the time of prayer comes, call the adhan and the iqama, and let the elder among you lead the prayer, and pray as you see me pray.'
187.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith, yaitu Abu Sulayman, bahwa ia dan seorang teman — atau dua teman — datang kepada Nabi ﷺ. Beliau berkata kepada mereka: 'Bila waktu shalat tiba, kumandangkan adzan dan iqamat, dan hendaklah yang paling tua di antara kalian mengimami shalat, dan shalatlah seperti yang kalian lihat aku shalat.'

188.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him), who was among the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Prophet ﷺ used to raise his hands when he entered prayer, when he bowed, and when he raised his head from bowing, until they were level with his ears.
188.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang termasuk sahabat Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa mengangkat kedua tangannya ketika memasuki shalat, ketika ruku', dan ketika mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku', hingga sejajar dengan telinganya.'

189.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that he used to come to their prayer place and converse with them. One day when prayer time came, they said: 'Come forward and lead us.' He said: 'Let one of you come forward. I will tell you why I will not lead you. I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever visits people should not lead them in prayer."'
189.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia biasa datang ke tempat shalat mereka dan berbincang-bincang dengan mereka. Suatu hari ketika waktu shalat tiba, mereka berkata: 'Majulah dan imamilah kami.' Ia berkata: 'Biarlah salah seorang dari kalian maju. Aku akan memberitahu kalian mengapa aku tidak akan mengimami kalian. Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa mengunjungi suatu kaum, hendaklah ia tidak mengimami mereka shalat."'

190.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he said the takbir, would raise his hands until they were close to his ears. When he bowed, he did the same. When he raised his head from bowing, he did the same.
190.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika bertakbir, mengangkat kedua tangannya hingga dekat dengan telinganya. Ketika ruku', beliau melakukan hal yang sama. Ketika mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku', beliau melakukan hal yang sama.'

191.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he entered prayer, raise his hands; and when he bowed; and when he raised his head from bowing — until they were level with the tops of his ears.'
191.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika memasuki shalat, mengangkat kedua tangannya; dan ketika ruku'; dan ketika mengangkat kepalanya dari ruku' — hingga sejajar dengan ujung telinganya.'

192.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to raise his hands level with the tops of his ears when bowing and prostrating.
192.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa mengangkat kedua tangannya sejajar dengan ujung telinganya ketika ruku' dan sujud.'

193.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (may Allah be pleased with him) that he used to come to their prayer place. When the prayer was called, they said to him: 'Come forward and pray with us.' He said: 'Let one of you pray, and I will tell you why I will not lead you.' When the people had prayed, he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When one of you visits a people, he should not lead them in prayer."'
193.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia biasa datang ke tempat shalat mereka. Ketika shalat dikumandangkan, mereka berkata kepadanya: 'Majulah dan shalatlah bersama kami.' Ia berkata: 'Biarlah salah seorang dari kalian shalat, dan aku akan memberitahu kalian mengapa aku tidak mengimami kalian.' Ketika orang-orang telah shalat, ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Apabila salah seorang dari kalian mengunjungi suatu kaum, hendaklah ia tidak mengimami mereka shalat."'

194.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Huwayrith al-Laythi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said to his companions one day: 'Shall I not show you how the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray?' — and it was not the time for prayer. He stood and made his standing complete, then bowed and made his bowing complete, then raised his head and stood upright for a moment, then prostrated, then raised himself.',
194.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Huwayrith al-Laythi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata kepada para sahabatnya suatu hari: 'Maukah aku tunjukkan kepada kalian bagaimana Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat?' — dan saat itu bukan waktu shalat. Ia berdiri dan menyempurnakan berdirinya, kemudian ruku' dan menyempurnakan ruku'nya, kemudian mengangkat kepalanya dan berdiri tegak sejenak, kemudian sujud, kemudian bangkit.'

195.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade the slingshot and said: 'It does not wound an enemy or catch prey, but it breaks teeth and gouges eyes.'
195.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang ketapel dan bersabda: 'Ia tidak melukai musuh dan tidak menangkap binatang buruan, tetapi memecahkan gigi dan mencungkil mata.'

196.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If prayer time comes and you are in the sheep pens, pray there. If prayer time comes and you are in the camel resting places, do not pray there, for they were created from devils.'
196.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika waktu shalat tiba dan kalian berada di kandang kambing, shalatlah di sana. Jika waktu shalat tiba dan kalian berada di tempat istirahat unta, janganlah shalat di sana, karena mereka diciptakan dari setan.'

197.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ recited Surah al-Fath in the year of the Conquest while traveling on his mount. Mu'awiya said: 'If I were not afraid that people would gather around me, I would recite it to you as he recited it' — with a melodious tone.
197.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ membaca Surah al-Fath pada tahun Penaklukan Makkah dalam perjalanannya di atas kendaraannya. Mu'awiyah berkata: 'Jika aku tidak khawatir orang-orang akan berkerumun di sekelilingku, aku akan membacakannya kepada kalian seperti beliau membacakannya' — dengan nada merdu.'

198.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Between every two calls to prayer there is an opportunity for prayer' — three times — 'for whoever wishes.'
198.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Di antara setiap dua azan terdapat kesempatan untuk shalat' — tiga kali — 'bagi siapa yang menghendaki.'

199.iwh_en: It was narrated from the son of 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal that he said: 'Our father, when he heard one of us say Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim, would say: Hmm, hmm. I prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, Abu Bakr, and 'Umar, and I did not hear any of them say Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim.'
199.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari putra 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal bahwa ia berkata: 'Ayah kami, ketika mendengar salah seorang dari kami mengucapkan Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim, akan berkata: Hmm, hmm. Aku shalat di belakang Rasulullah ﷺ, Abu Bakar, dan 'Umar, dan aku tidak mendengar seorang pun dari mereka mengucapkan Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim.'

200.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him), who was one of those about whom the verse was revealed — 'and not upon those who, when they came to you so that you could provide them with mounts' — that he said: 'I was holding a branch of the tree to shade the Prophet ﷺ while they were pledging allegiance to him, and they said.'
200.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Mughaffal (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang merupakan salah seorang dari mereka yang tentangnya diturunkan ayat — 'dan tidak pula atas orang-orang yang apabila mereka datang kepadamu agar engkau memberi mereka kendaraan' — bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku memegang sebuah cabang pohon untuk menaungi Nabi ﷺ sementara mereka sedang membai'at beliau, dan mereka berkata.'
